content.problemStatement:
Rafid is teaching his students plane geometry. Today they are talking about convex polygons. A convex polygon is a simple non-degenerate polygon such that each of its internal angles is strictly smaller than 180∘180^\circ180∘.

Rafid drew NNN points (numbered 111 through NNN) on a blackboard (a two-dimensional plane) and told each student to choose some of the points and connect them to obtain a convex polygon. For each valid iii, the coordinates of the iii-th point are (Xi,Yi)(X_i, Y_i)(Xi​,Yi​).

There are KKK students (numbered 111 through KKK). To prevent the students from choosing the same polygon over and over, Rafid told them that they have to choose polygons one by one in the order from student 111 to student KKK and each student has to choose a set of points which was not chosen by any earlier student. That means they have to choose KKK different sets of points, resulting in KKK different polygons. As a result, some students may end up unable to choose any polygon.

To encourage the students, Rafid announced that each student will receive a number of candies proportional to the area of this student's polygon. The students are very greedy, so the first student will choose the polygon with the largest area, the second student will choose the polygon with the largest area which does not use the same set of points as the first student's polygon, and so on.

For each student, help Rafid find the area of the polygon chosen by this student or determine that this student cannot choose a polygon anymore. Note that while there may be many ways in which the students could choose their polygons, their areas are uniquely determined.
constraints: the points (X1,Y1),(X2,Y2),…,(XN,YN)(X_1, Y_1), (X_2, Y_2), \ldots, (X_N, Y_N)(X1​,Y1​),(X2​,Y2​),…,(XN​,YN​) are pairwise distinct
it is possible to form at least KKK convex polygons
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers XiX_iXi​ and YiY_iYi​.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing KKK space-separated integers A1,A2,…,AKA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_KA1​,A2​,…,AK​. For each valid iii, AiA_iAi​ should be twice the area of a polygon chosen by the iii-th student, or −1-1−1 if all possible polygons have already been chosen by students before the iii-th student.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The following figure describes the points and some polygons.


The first student chooses the largest polygon ABCD\mathsf{ABCD}ABCD with area 100100100.
The next four students choose the triangles ABC\mathsf{ABC}ABC, BCD\mathsf{BCD}BCD, CDA\mathsf{CDA}CDA and DAB\mathsf{DAB}DAB, each with area 505050. Note that for example, the polygon AECB\mathsf{AECB}AECB is not convex because it has one internal angle equal to 180∘180^{\circ}180∘.
The next four students choose the triangles AEB\mathsf{AEB}AEB, BEC\mathsf{BEC}BEC, CED\mathsf{CED}CED and DEA\mathsf{DEA}DEA, each with area 252525.
There are no more polygons the students can choose, so the remaining six students are left without a polygon.
